content.problemStatement:
Chef invented a modified wordle.

There is a hidden word SSS and a guess word TTT, both of length 555.

Chef defines a string MMM to determine the correctness of the guess word. For the ithi^{th}ith index:


If the guess at the ithi^{th}ith index is correct, the ithi^{th}ith character of MMM is G\texttt{G}G.
If the guess at the ithi^{th}ith index is wrong, the ithi^{th}ith character of MMM is B\texttt{B}B.
Given the hidden word SSS and guess TTT, determine string MMM.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains of two lines of input.
First line contains the string SSS - the hidden word.
Second line contains the string TTT - the guess word.


Output Format
For each test case, print the value of string MMM.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings BgBgB\texttt{BgBgB}BgBgB, BGBGB\texttt{BGBGB}BGBGB, bgbGB\texttt{bgbGB}bgbGB and bgbgb\texttt{bgbgb}bgbgb will all be treated as identical).
constraints: length of S = length of T = 5
S and T contain only uppercase English letters
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains the hidden word S (a string of length 5 consisting of uppercase English letters), the second line contains the guess word T (also length 5, uppercase).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a string M of length 5 on its own line. M[i] is 'G' if S[i] equals T[i], otherwise 'B'. Upper‑ or lower‑case letters are both accepted.

sampleTestCases.input:
3
ABCDE
EDCBA
ROUND
RINGS
START
STUNT
sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: compare ABCDE with EDCBA → positions 3 match → BBGBB.\nTest case 2: ROUND vs RINGS → only first position matches → GBBBB.\nTest case 3: START vs STUNT → positions 1,2,5 match → GGBBG.
First test: all characters equal → GGGGG. Second test: no character matches → BBBBB.
HELLO vs HEAVY → first two positions match → GGBBB. WORLD vs WORMS → first three positions match → GGGBB.

solution.approach: For each test case iterate over the five positions. Compare the characters of S and T at the same index. Append 'G' to the answer if they are equal, otherwise append 'B'. After processing all five positions output the built string. The algorithm is O(5) per test case, i.e., O(T) overall, and uses O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each test case processes exactly 5 characters.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used regardless of input size.

code.Python3:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()  # first token is T, then pairs of strings
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        s = next(it)
        t = next(it)
        ans = ['G' if a == b else 'B' for a, b in zip(s, t)]
        out_lines.append(''.join(ans))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
